title: Longest Word in Dictionary
description:
Longest Word in Dictionary

Given an array of strings words representing an English dictionary, return the longest word in words that can be built one character at a time by other words in words.

If there is more than one possible answer, return the longest word with the smallest lexicographical order. If there is no answer, return "".

Examples:

  • Input: words = ["w","wo","wor","worl","world"]
    Output: "world"
    Explanation: The word "world" can be built one character at a time by "w", "wo", "wor", and "worl", then "world".
  • Input: words = ["a","banana","app","appl","ap","apply","apple"]
    Output: "apple"
    Explanation: Both "apply" and "apple" can be built from other words in the dictionary, but "apple" is lexicographically smaller than "apply".

Constraints:

  • 1 <= words.length <= 1000
  • 1 <= words[i].length <= 30
  • words[i] consists of lowercase English letters
solution_text:
Longest Word in Dictionary

Algorithm:

  1. Put all words in a set for O(1) prefix lookups
  2. Iterate words in sorted order (lexicographic)
  3. A word is valid if it has length 1 or its prefix without the last character is in the set
  4. Keep the best word by longer length, breaking ties with lexicographically smaller string

Time Complexity: O(n log n + m) for sorting and checking, where n is number of words and m total characters

Space Complexity: O(n) for the set
